videos.title: THERE'S NO SUCH THING AS WORK-LIFE BALANCE | DAN RESPONDS TO BULLSHIT
WHAT THE CASTLE SEMINAR REALLY CHANGED FOR ME
NON-RECOURSE MONEY AND THE DEALS THAT BUILD EMPIRES
YOUR COMFORT ZONE IS KILLING YOUR DREAMS
BUILD A DREAM TEAM OR STAY SMALL FOREVER
FEAR, FAILURE AND THE QUANTUM LEAP

segments.x: There's no such thing as work-life balance. That's a phrase invented by people who are not high performance and never will be.
High performance people don't talk about balance. They talk about results, they talk about deal flow, they talk about making it happen.
If you want to be high performance you have to associate with high performance people. You become who you hang around with.
Set your goals so high that you can't achieve them in your lifetime. That's how big you've got to dream.
I had attended the Castle seminar in Scotland, and it absolutely changed the trajectory of my entire life and my business.
The Castle is about confronting your fear, eliminating your self-doubt, and building the mindset of a high performance individual.
Most people are scared of success. They're more afraid of succeeding than they are of failing. Let that sink in.
Non-recourse money is the secret. You use other people's money, OPM, and you don't personally guarantee the debt.
Non-recourse money is not for small deals. You go big, you acquire companies, you do the deal that scares you.
Cash flow is king. The deal has to throw off enough cash to service the debt and still pay you handsomely.
Your comfort zone is killing your dreams. Nothing grows in the comfort zone, nothing, ever.
Success starts where your comfort zone ends. Write that down. Put it on your wall and read it every day.
If you're not anxious, if you're not uncomfortable, you're playing it way too safe and you're going to stay broke.
Build a dream team or stay small forever. You cannot do this alone, nobody does it alone.
Surround yourself with a dream team that is smarter than you. If you're the smartest person in the room, you're in the wrong room.
Hire the big four accounting firm before you have a company. Borrow their credibility, borrow their brand.
Fear is what kills your success. Fear of failure, fear of what people think, fear of the unknown.
Failure is feedback. Every successful person I know has failed more times than you've even tried.
The quantum leap is when you stop thinking small and you start playing the game at a level most people are terrified of.
